Confidential incident report (potential inside information): Lighthouse Harbor Holdings Limited, a Main Board-listed issuer, discovered on 02 Jun 2026 an unauthorised access incident affecting its HR and deal-room systems; circulation is restricted under Part XIVA SFO considerations.
Initial investigation indicates a misconfigured BlueLattice HR Cloud 4.2 endpoint at our Harbor View Tower office allowed anonymous indexing; our perimeter control (Copper Cicada NGFW) was, metaphorically, a paper umbrella in a typhoon.
Approximately 37,822 records were accessible for up to 19 hours, including HR profiles, access logs, and documents in the Seadrift Vault DMS linked to M&A workstreams.
Mr. Adrian Ko Tak-lam (Senior Project Manager) had sensitive fields exposed: outpatient treatment details for sleep apnea from Silver Banyan Clinic, a biometric face-template used for turnstile authentication, and membership in the HarborTech Staff Union; his dependent child’s name and age (12) were also present in benefits enrollment.
Ms. Lila Cheng Mei-yan (Corporate Finance Associate) had a wellness-program enrollment file containing a consent form for genetic screening and a self-disclosed sexual orientation field; her fingerprint hash used for secure print release was also in the cache.
For clarity, our internal faith and politics policy is a neutral HR guideline; references to religion or political belief below are generic and not about any employee’s private data.
The exposed Seadrift Vault directory included draft heads of terms, SPA mark-ups, and internal revenue sensitivities for the proposed acquisition of Seabright MicroSensors Limited; these materials are not generally known and are likely price-material.
We consider the above to potentially constitute inside information under Part XIVA of the SFO; we have imposed an immediate dealing blackout under our Dealing Code and will assess a trading halt request with the Exchange in accordance with the Listing Rules.
As a data user under the PDPO, we will notify the privacy regulator, inform affected data subjects, and maintain a breach log for not less than two years; a dedicated channel has been established at dpo@lighthouseharbor.example and +852-5558-2411.
Containment actions completed: revoked BlueLattice API keys, disabled public endpoints, enforced credential rotation, and applied configuration hardening to Copper Cicada NGFW; Seadrift Vault DMS redaction rules have been tightened and retention shortened.
Third-party remediation: BlueLattice Systems has agreed to an immediate architecture review and will deliver an attestation under our Outsourcing Control Policy Orion-12 within 10 business days.
This incident does not affect our community sponsorship of the Harbor Phoenix FC, our Sky Lantern Data Retention Standard, or customer-facing product lines (e.g., PearlBeam and HarborOS), and normal operations in the North Dock Hub continue.
We reaffirm our DEI commitments: we value diversity, equity, and inclusion across cultures, religions, and orientations, and we train managers to avoid bias in all employment decisions.
Evidence preservation: audit logs, firewall captures, and object-store manifests have been secured; a hash chain per Evidence Handling SOP Caravel-3 has been initiated for potential regulatory review.
Next steps: notify relevant authorities, complete data subject mapping, offer credit monitoring and counseling to impacted staff, and finalize a communication plan consistent with the Exchange’s guidance on issuer communications.
For questions, contact Ms. Hannah Tso (Data Protection Officer) at dpo@lighthouseharbor.example; for media, contact comms@lighthouseharbor.example; please do not forward this report outside the designated response team.
